scenarios.rationale: AIX 7.2 is EOL. IBM AIX 8.x or migration to a supported OS is required. However, AIX-to-Linux OS update for COBOL is non-trivial.
Migration from proprietary AIX to RHEL/Ubuntu would reduce vendor lock-in and eliminate AIX license costs. Requires COBOL runtime porting.
AIX runs on IBM POWER architecture. ARM migration would require complete platform rewrite. Not feasible without full application modernization first.
ERPApp-001 has no application server (1-Tier COBOL). Scenario not applicable.
On-Premise ERP could be migrated to cloud (e.g., IBM Cloud for AIX or lift-and-shift with COBOL runtime). Significant effort required.
COBOL on AIX cannot be containerized without first modernizing the application to a containerizable runtime. Blocked by legacy stack.
1-Tier COBOL monolith is the primary candidate for refactoring. Strangler-fig pattern or incremental modernization could decouple financial modules.
Oracle 19c is CURRENT_VERSION with support until 2027. No immediate database upgrade needed.
Oracle 19c could be migrated to Oracle Cloud or Amazon RDS to eliminate on-premise DB server management.
Oracle 19c is not available on ARM managed services without an engine change. Not applicable in current state.
Oracle database workload for core ERP financials is not suitable for serverless database patterns.
Migration from Oracle 19c to PostgreSQL would eliminate Oracle licensing costs. High effort due to Oracle-specific SQL/PL-SQL features in ERP.
